senses.definition: Medical: a condition where a person is partly or completely unable to see, often because of a problem in the eye or visual system.
examples: The patient developed anopia after the stroke affected the visual centers of the brain.
Anopia can range from small blind spots to almost total blindness in one eye.
Doctors ordered brain imaging to find the cause of his sudden anopia.

senses.definition: Plural of anopia: several cases or types of medical conditions where vision is partly or completely lost.
examples: The textbook describes different anopias linked to damage in specific parts of the brain.
Clinicians must distinguish among various anopias when examining the visual field.
Researchers are mapping how different anopias relate to particular nerve injuries.

senses.definition: A manuscript, parchment, or book that has writing or printing on only one side of each leaf.
examples: The museum holds an early anopisthograph from the medieval period.
Because it was an anopisthograph, the reverse pages were left completely blank.
Scholars examined the anopisthograph to understand early bookmaking practices.

senses.definition: Having writing or printing on only one side of a sheet, leaf, or page.
examples: The scroll is anopisthographic, so the back remains unmarked.
Anopisthographic documents are common in certain ancient collections.
The archivist noted that most of the letters in this box are anopisthographic.

senses.definition: In a way that involves writing or printing on only one side of a sheet or page.
examples: The text is printed anopisthographically to protect the fragile paper.
These records were originally produced anopisthographically for easier reading.
Manuscripts were often copied anopisthographically in early scriptoria.

senses.definition: A former zoological group of ribbon worms that lack certain spines on the head, used mainly in scientific classification.
examples: The class Anopla includes ribbon worms with a simple head structure.
Some older textbooks still refer to Anopla as a separate group.
Researchers debated whether Anopla should be kept as a distinct division.

senses.definition: Having no pubic hair, used mainly as a medical or descriptive anatomical term.
examples: The condition left the patient anoplanthus from adolescence.
In some syndromes, individuals may be anoplanthus as well as generally hairless.
The report briefly mentioned anoplanthus appearance in the genital region.

senses.definition: Relating to or infected with tapeworms of the genus Anoplocephala, especially in veterinary medicine.
examples: The horse showed signs of anoplocephalic infestation.
Anoplocephalic infections are treated with specific antiparasitic drugs.
The vet examined the gut for anoplocephalic segments.

senses.definition: Relating to a subgroup of ribbon worms that lack certain armature, in zoological classification.
examples: The anoplonemertean form is distinguished by its simple anterior end.
Anoplonemertean worms are often found in marine environments.
The researcher identified several anoplonemertean species in the sample.

senses.definition: A taxonomic group of ribbon worms characterized by the lack of certain head spines, used in scientific classification.
examples: Anoplonemertini are typically marine and often brightly colored.
The paper proposes a revision of the genus within Anoplonemertini.
Several new species of Anoplonemertini were described last year.

senses.definition: An extinct hoofed mammal of the genus Anoplotherium from the Eocene and Oligocene periods.
examples: The anoplothere was a small, deer-like animal that lived in ancient Europe.
Fossils of anoplotheres have been found in several parts of France.
Paleontologists reconstructed the likely appearance of the anoplothere.

senses.definition: A family of extinct hoofed mammals that includes the genus Anoplotherium.
examples: Members of Anoplotheriidae were common in early European fossil sites.
The Anoplotheriidae show adaptations for browsing on soft vegetation.
Scientists debate the exact relationships of Anoplotheriidae within ungulates.

senses.definition: Resembling or related to the extinct mammals of the family Anoplotheriidae.
examples: The fossil displays several anoplotherioid characteristics.
Anoplotherioid traits suggest a close relationship with early ungulates.
Researchers described the jaw as distinctly anoplotherioid in form.

senses.definition: A genus of extinct hoofed mammals, known from Eocene and Oligocene fossils in Europe.
examples: Anoplotherium is one of the best-known early ungulate genera.
Cuvier first described Anoplotherium based on fossils from France.
The museum exhibits a nearly complete Anoplotherium skeleton.

senses.definition: Having features similar to those of Anoplotherium or related extinct mammals.
examples: The fossil jaw is distinctly anoplotheroid in its shape.
Anoplotheroid features point to a browsing lifestyle.
Several anoplotheroid species have been identified at the site.

senses.definition: A group or order of insects that includes the sucking lice which feed on the blood of mammals.
examples: Anoplura includes many lice that are parasites of humans and animals.
The biology of Anoplura is important for controlling livestock diseases.
Students learned to recognize members of Anoplura under the microscope.

senses.definition: Having a form or structure similar to members of Anoplura (sucking lice).
examples: The specimen has an anopluriform body with narrow, elongated segments.
Anopluriform mouthparts are adapted for piercing skin and sucking blood.
Researchers described a new anopluriform parasite of rodents.

senses.definition: A defect or loss of vision in part or all of the visual field, usually because of damage in the visual system.
examples: The neurologist diagnosed anopsia after testing the patient's visual fields.
Anopsia in one half of the field is called hemianopsia.
Lesions in the optic tract can result in specific patterns of anopsia.

senses.definition: Plural of anopsia: several kinds or cases of visual field loss.
examples: Different anopsias can indicate damage at different levels of the visual pathway.
The chart illustrates common anopsias observed after stroke.
Clinicians distinguish among various anopsias using perimetry tests.

senses.definition: A rare or variant form referring to loss or defect of vision, similar to anopsia.
examples: The specialist used the term anopsy to describe the visual deficit.
Cases of anopsy may involve only part of the visual field.
Anopsy is discussed briefly in older neurology texts.

senses.definition: Having no pubic hair, used mainly as a medical or anatomical description.
examples: The disorder left the patient completely anopubic.
Some endocrine conditions can result in anopubic appearance.
The report noted that the child was anopubic despite being of pubertal age.

senses.definition: A short, warm jacket with a hood that protects you from wind and rain.
examples: She put on her anorak before going out into the storm.
A waterproof anorak is essential for hiking in this region.
He bought a thick anorak for the winter trip.

senses.definition: British informal: a person who is very interested in the details of a subject that most other people find boring.
examples: He is a real train anorak who spends weekends at the railway museum.
Football anoraks love to quote old match statistics.
She used to be an astronomy anorak at school.

senses.definition: Plural of anorak: several jackets with hoods that protect from wind and rain.
examples: The shop sells anoraks in many different colors.
They packed their anoraks in case the weather turned cold.
Children hung their wet anoraks by the door.

senses.definition: Plural of anorak (British informal): people who are overly interested in a narrow subject seen as boring by others.
examples: The club is full of model-train anoraks.
To outsiders they look like anoraks, but their knowledge is impressive.
Some music anoraks collect every version of a single song.

senses.definition: A condition in which both testes are absent in a male, usually because they did not develop.
examples: The infant was diagnosed with anorchi after imaging showed no testicular tissue.
Anorchi is often associated with hormonal abnormalities.
Endocrinologists manage patients with anorchi through hormone replacement.

senses.definition: A medical condition in which a male has no testes, usually because they failed to form or were lost very early.
examples: Anorchia is usually discovered when a child fails to go through puberty.
Hormone tests can confirm a diagnosis of anorchia.
Patients with anorchia often require lifelong hormone therapy.

senses.definition: The state or condition of having no testes.
examples: Anorchism may be caused by problems in fetal development.
Endocrine evaluation is essential in suspected anorchism.
The article reviews the causes and treatment of anorchism.

senses.definition: Having no testes, used as a medical or anatomical description.
examples: The patient is anorchous due to a congenital condition.
Anorchous individuals require careful hormonal monitoring.
Anorchous status was confirmed by ultrasound and blood tests.

senses.definition: A male who has no testes, usually because of a medical condition present from birth.
examples: The clinic offers counseling for anorchus patients and their families.
As an anorchus, he needed hormone therapy during adolescence.
The study followed the development of several anorchus cases over time.

senses.definition: Relating to the anus and the rectum.
examples: The patient suffered from an anorectal infection.
Anorectal disorders can cause pain and bleeding.
Surgery was required to correct the anorectal malformation.

senses.definition: Relating to or causing loss of appetite, especially as a medical effect.
examples: The drug has anorectic properties and reduces food intake.
Anorectic agents are sometimes prescribed for obesity.
Some antidepressants can have anorectic side effects.

senses.definition: A person, especially a patient, who has anorexia nervosa or a serious loss of appetite.
examples: The clinic provides support for anorectics and their families.
As an anorectic, she struggled to maintain a healthy weight.
Doctors warned that the anorectic was at risk of heart problems.

senses.definition: Plural of anorectic: drugs that reduce appetite.
examples: Doctors sometimes prescribe anorectics for short-term weight loss.
Abuse of anorectics can lead to serious heart problems.
Newer anorectics act on specific brain receptors.

senses.definition: Plural of anorectic: people who have anorexia nervosa or serious loss of appetite.
examples: The unit specializes in treating young anorectics.
Support groups for anorectics meet weekly.
Doctors stress early intervention for anorectics.

senses.definition: Showing or affected by loss of appetite.
examples: The child was anorectous and refused most meals.
Anorectous patients need careful nutritional support.
She remained anorectous despite treatment.

senses.definition: Causing loss of appetite, or affected by severe loss of appetite.
examples: The medication has anoretic effects at higher doses.
Anoretic behavior in teenagers should be taken seriously.
Some patients become anoretic after major surgery.

senses.definition: A person with anorexia nervosa or severe loss of appetite.
examples: The clinic treats both bulimics and anoretics.
Anoretics often need long-term psychological support.
Parents were worried that their daughter was becoming an anoretic.

senses.definition: Plural of anoretic: drugs or substances that reduce appetite.
examples: Some anoretics were withdrawn from the market because of side effects.
Researchers study how anoretics act on brain chemistry.
Doctors warn patients not to buy unregulated anoretics online.

senses.definition: Plural of anoretic: people who have anorexia nervosa or severe loss of appetite.
examples: The ward has several young anoretics under close observation.
Support groups for anoretics are an important part of treatment.
Doctors stress the high medical risk for chronic anoretics.

senses.definition: A serious eating disorder in which a person refuses to eat enough and becomes dangerously thin.
examples: She was treated for anorexia throughout her teenage years.
Anorexia can affect both physical and mental health.
Early support can improve recovery from anorexia.

senses.definition: Medical: loss of appetite for food.
examples: The patient reported anorexia and weight loss over two months.
Anorexia is a common symptom in many chronic illnesses.
Fever and pain often lead to temporary anorexia.

senses.definition: A drug or substance that reduces appetite.
examples: Anorexiants were once widely used for weight loss.
The doctor prescribed a mild anorexiant for a short period.
Some anorexiants have been linked to heart problems.

senses.definition: Having the effect of reducing appetite.
examples: The compound has anorexiant properties in animal studies.
Anorexiant medications should be used with caution.
They are developing a safer anorexiant drug.

senses.definition: Plural of anorexia: several cases or types of eating disorders marked by strong restriction of food intake.
examples: Clinicians presented different anorexias seen in their practice.
The book describes how various anorexias may develop in adolescence.
Research into the causes of anorexias is ongoing.

senses.definition: Having anorexia nervosa, or very little desire to eat.
examples: She looked painfully thin and anorexic.
Anorexic patients often deny that they are underweight.
The doctor was concerned about his increasingly anorexic appearance.

senses.definition: A person who has anorexia nervosa.
examples: The clinic specializes in helping anorexics and bulimics.
As an anorexic, she struggled with food and body image.
Many anorexics hide their illness for a long time.

senses.definition: Plural of anorexic: people who have anorexia nervosa.
examples: The hospital offers a program for anorexics and their families.
Young anorexics often need long-term therapy.
Doctors say many anorexics also suffer from depression.

senses.definition: Plural of anorexia: several instances or types of anorexia as an eating disorder or loss of appetite.
examples: The study compares different anorexies across cultures.
Some anorexies are linked to cultural pressure to be thin.
Doctors discussed how to treat various anorexies effectively.

senses.definition: Causing a loss of appetite or reducing the desire to eat.
examples: The hormone has an anorexigenic effect on the brain.
Researchers are studying anorexigenic signals in the gut.
Anorexigenic drugs must be used cautiously.

senses.definition: A less common spelling of anorexia, meaning a serious eating disorder or loss of appetite.
examples: Older medical texts sometimes use the term anorexy.
Her anorexy led to dangerous weight loss.
Doctors treated his anorexy with therapy and medication.

senses.definition: Substances or materials that are not part of living organisms, considered as non-organic matter.
examples: The chemist distinguished between organica and anorgana in the sample.
Anorgana include minerals and many synthetic compounds.
The study focuses on the interaction of anorgana with biological systems.

senses.definition: Inorganic; not consisting of or derived from living matter.
examples: Anorganic salts make up a large part of the rock.
The sample contained both organic and anorganic components.
Anorganic materials are studied in inorganic chemistry.

senses.definition: A theoretical or philosophical term for something that is not a living organism.
examples: He contrasted the living organism with the inert anorganism.
In the text, anorganism represents pure matter without life.
The philosopher used anorganism to discuss the origins of life.

senses.definition: The study of inorganic matter, especially in contrast to living organisms.
examples: Anorganology developed alongside early inorganic chemistry.
The book traces the history of anorganology in European science.
His research moves between anorganology and biology.

senses.definition: Not normal; abnormal, often used as a variant or less common form of abnormal.
examples: The report notes anormal development of the limb.
Anormal behavior in children may signal distress.
He showed anormal reactions to stress.

senses.definition: The state of being not normal; an abnormal condition.
examples: Doctors investigated the anormality seen on the scan.
Any anormality should be checked by a specialist.
The test results showed no anormality in liver function.

senses.definition: Without ornaments or decoration; plain or undecorated.
examples: The building stood anorn against the grey sky.
She preferred anorn designs with clean lines.
The room was anorn, with bare walls and simple furniture.

senses.definition: Not related to mountain-building processes; not formed by compression of the Earth's crust.
examples: The region is characterized by anorogenic igneous activity.
Anorogenic granites often form within stable continental interiors.
He studied anorogenic events in the geological record.
